content.assessment: As a no-new-policy counterfactual, this proposal requires no active implementation capacity, sequencing, or monitoring design by definition, which trivially satisfies deliverability but only because there are no actors executing a mechanism to assess. The lens evidence confirms the proposal's own stated risk profile: demographic contraction (F-live-implementation_planning-08) will interact with statutory class-size minimums unevenly across geography, and the existing pattern of church-sector growth outside state jurisdiction (F-live-implementation_planning-05) suggests that passive public-sector contraction could be offset by relative expansion of an unregulated selective channel, exactly the failure-containment gap this lens is designed to flag. Since no active mechanism exists to monitor or adapt to this displacement, this baseline scenario has zero built-in capacity to detect or correct for it, which is a structural rather than incidental weakness of the counterfactual.
opportunities: Because no active policy is required, this baseline could still be paired retroactively with monitoring instruments to track church-sector share and geographic divergence, even though none is specified
strengths: Requires no actor coordination, workforce redeployment, or new governance capacity, since no active mechanism is being implemented
Provides a lower-bound comparison against which the delivery costs of active proposals can be assessed
threats: Documented church-sector scale and admissions discretion (F-live-implementation_planning-05) creates a strong likelihood that passive state-sector contraction is offset by relative growth of an unregulated selective channel, with no corrective mechanism available under a no-new-policy baseline
Uneven demographic contraction (F-live-implementation_planning-08) could produce urban-rural divergence in selectivity that persists indefinitely absent any policy lever to address it
weaknesses: Contains no monitoring or adaptation mechanism whatsoever, since it is explicitly the absence of policy, meaning any adverse distributional shift (e.g., urban-rural divergence) would proceed fully undetected by any dedicated feedback loop

Irregular demographic age pyramids (F-live-implementation_planning-08) mean the contraction this proposal relies on will not proceed smoothly or predictably, undermining its implicit assumption of gradual, manageable decline
